title: A delimitation without discipline: What the failure of the 131st Amendment reveals about constitutional control
description: For nearly five decades, India managed to avoid a difficult constitutional question: how to balance equal representation with the demands of federal balance. The freeze on delimitation was not an act of avoidance; it was a deliberate constitutional compromise.That compromise returned to the centre of discussion with the introduction of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026. The proposal aimed to expand the Lok Sabha and restart the delimitation exercise. The Bill has now failed to secure
